action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent ModuleNotFoundError for xgboost across iters 8-14 despite code in model.py/train.py/predict.py being ready (XGBRegressor with MAE objective, early_stopping on val, Morgan FP r=3 2048bit, n_est=1000); best MAE still 0.2188 from RF (MLP worse at 0.231). Iter_7 curve shows smooth convergence but FP+tree limit. Analysis: xgboost not preinstalled (allowed via packages_to_install which persists); once installed, XGB with hist method + early_stop + MAE objective should outperform RF (~0.16-0.19 expected) on high-dim sparse tabular data. FP calc (~3-5min) + training acceptable with 1.81h/16 iters left. No code changes needed. Decision: Install xgboost, run current train.py (full data, logs val_mae to metrics.jsonl, saves best.pkl), submit_eval for test MAE + timing before next opts (e.g. Mordred feats, more rounds, or 3D GNN).
